commands.review_summary:
The PR bounds the successful OpenRouter OAuth API-key response read with `readResponseWithLimit` and adds an oversized-response regression test.

PR surface: Source +7, Tests +38. Total +45 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main calls `response.text()` for successful OpenRouter OAuth key responses, and existing repo tests document that bare `response.text()` buffers oversized bodies. I did not execute tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated code repair is needed; the remaining action is contributor-supplied real behavior proof or an explicit maintainer proof override.

target.title: fix(openrouter): bound OAuth API key response read in readResponseBody to prevent OOM
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The `readResponseBody` function in the OpenRouter OAuth flow uses `await response.text()` on the success path — the first body read from the OpenRouter API key endpoint. This is unbounded and can cause OOM on oversized responses. The error path already uses `readResponseTextLimited` with an 8 KB cap, but the success path was unprotected.

This replaces the success path `response.text()` with `readResponseWithLimit` (16 MiB cap), matching the codebase convention.

## Evidence

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22, OpenClaw main @ 0ce10d7793

**Existing tests**: 8/8 pass (PKCE URL, redirect parse, code exchange, error messages, error body bounding, browser OAuth, callback order, metadata unchanged).

**New regression test**: 1 oversized-response test passes:
- `rejects oversized OpenRouter OAuth API key responses` — verifies the key exchange path catches overflow from an 18 MiB response stream before full buffering

**Test approach**: Provides a `fetchImpl` mock returning an 18 MiB ReadableStream (cap is 16 MiB), asserts the error propagates with `"OpenRouter OAuth key response exceeds"`.

## Risk

Low. The 16 MiB cap matches the codebase convention. The bounded read replaces the exact same `response.text()` call on the success path; the error path already uses `readResponseTextLimited` and is unchanged.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: cxbasdev
